source_jobs.title: Sr. Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



About the Role

We’re looking for a Senior Software Engineer who thrives in an AI-native environment—someone who’s excited to build scalable, intelligent systems that blend automation, reasoning, and modern infrastructure.

You’ll play a key role in architecting and implementing the systems that power our AI-driven platform, helping define how we integrate large language models (LLMs), knowledge graphs, and intelligent workflows into enterprise-scale software.

This is a hands-on role for someone who codes deeply, thinks systemically, and wants to shape the future of AI-augmented engineering.

Responsibilities

AI-Driven Technical Ownership

 - Take end-to-end ownership of complex features and systems—from architecture and implementation to deployment and observability.

 - Design and evolve AI-native services that embed LLMs and reasoning capabilities directly into product workflows.

 - Work cross-functionally with product, design, and data teams to infuse AI-powered intelligence into the user and developer experience.

 - Drive architectural decisions that balance rapid experimentation with long-term scalability and reliability.

Hands-On Engineering

 - Backend First: Lead design, implementation, testing, and deployment of backend services, APIs (REST/GraphQL), and core data infrastructure that power scalable enterprise workflows.

 - Full Stack Delivery: Build user-facing features where needed, connecting frontend UI with backend systems — you’ll own the full path from browser to database.

 - Shape the Stack: Help define and evolve our architecture — from backend systems and databases to the UI frameworks and client integrations that consume them.

 - AI and Workflow Innovation: Integrate AI/LLM capabilities into backend pipelines and product logic that make ITSM smarter and easier to use.

 - Performance & Scale: Architect highly performant, secure, and scalable services for enterprise environments, with monitoring and observability in place.

 - Collaboration: Partner with designers, frontend engineers, and infrastructure teams to translate product thinking into real, high-impact solutions.

 - Developer Experience: Improve internal tooling, deployment pipelines, and best practices around CI/CD and automated testing.

 - Security & Compliance: Build with enterprise-grade security and compliance in mind from the start (SOC 2, ISO 27001).

Qualifications

 - Experience: Strong background in backend systems and API design with real SaaS production experience; comfortable contributing across the stack.

 - Backend Languages: Proficiency in one or more modern backend languages (Python, Go, Rust, C++, etc.) and designing distributed systems.

 - Frontend Fluency: Familiar with modern frontend frameworks (React, Vue, Angular) and comfortable building or iterating on UI components that interface with your backend.

 - Databases & Storage: Strong hands-on experience with NoSQL and (ideally) graph or databases.

 - Cloud & DevOps: Experience with cloud platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure), containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and infrastructure as code (Terraform).

 - Problem Solving Mindset: Pragmatic, product-driven engineer who thrives in high-velocity startup environments and can balance backend complexity with UI quality.

 - Bonus: Experience integrating AI/LLM features or working with enterprise workflow systems.

Nice to Have

 - Experience building microservices or event-driven architectures.

 - Prior experience with high-throughput or low-latency systems.

 - Contributions to open-source projects or a strong portfolio of technical work.

Why You’ll Love This Role

 - Build core systems for an AI-native enterprise platform—w

source_jobs.title: Visual Designer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is a stealth-mode, AI-native startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



About the Role

We’re looking for a Visual Designer to own and shape our brand identity from day one — including developing our company logo and building a cohesive brand system across digital touch points, i.e. website design. This is an in-office position in New York City (5 days per week) working directly with the founding team.

Responsibilities

 - Design and develop the company logo and establish brand usage guidelines

 - Build and maintain a cohesive digital-first brand identity system

 - Create marketing collateral (presentations, web/digital assets)

 - Collaborate with product/engineering on UI/UX aesthetics and web design

 - Design and produce web graphics, landing pages, and digital campaigns
   

Qualifications

 - Degree in Graphic Design/Visual Communication from a top-tier design school (e.g., RISD, SCAD, Parsons, Pratt, SVA, RCA, Central Saint Martins, Design Academy Eindhoven, etc.)

 - 2–5 years of professional branding, digital design and software experience (agency, startup, or in-house)

 - Proven portfolio in digital/web design, branding, and logo creation

 - Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite + Figma

 - Comfortable switching between AI and non-AI tools for design work

 - Motion design, illustration, or 3D a plus
   

What We Offer

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office, where you’ll be side-by-side with the founding team in a supportive, collaborative setting. Our team works on-site five days a week, growing and building together, and the location is easy to reach with plenty of public transportation options.

 - Define the brand identity and develop the company logo from the ground up

 - Competitive salary + equity

 - Full health, dental, and vision benefits

 - Ambitious, collaborative team culture in NYC


As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at is: $90,000—$130,000 USD.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



About the Role
We are seeking Full Stack Engineers with deep experience designing and building enterprise-grade applications. You’ll be responsible for building user-facing features and backend services end-to-end, collaborating closely with designers, product engineers, and infrastructure experts.

Responsibilities

 - Own features end-to-end: design, implement, test, and deploy user-facing functionality and backend APIs

 - Shape the stack: work across front-end, backend, and database layers (stack not set yet — you’ll help define it)

 - AI-native product development: integrate AI/LLM capabilities into workflows to make ITSM smarter and more efficient

 - Performance & scale: build highly performant, secure, and scalable services for enterprise customers

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: partner with platform engineers on infrastructure, and with product/design on UX

 - Developer productivity: contribute to internal tooling, code quality, and CI/CD best practices

 - Security & compliance: bake in enterprise-grade security and compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001) from day one

 - Customer impact: deliver features that enterprise users actually love, in contrast to the legacy tools they tolerate

Qualifications

 - Education: Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field from a top-tier engineering school

 - Experience: Full Stack Engineering (or Software Engineer with end-to-end ownership) in SaaS environments serving enterprise customers

 - Frontend: Skilled with modern frameworks (React, Vue, Angular, or similar). Strong understanding of design systems and building clean, performant UIs

 - Backend: Proficient in at least one modern programming language (Python, Go, Rust, C++ or similar). Experience designing REST/GraphQL APIs and distributed systems

 - Databases: Hands-on with relational (Postgres/MySQL) and ideally non-relational or graph databases

 - Cloud: Strong experience with AWS, GCP, or Azure

 - DevOps: Familiar with CI/CD pipelines, Docker, Kubernetes, and IaC (Terraform)

 - Mindset: Pragmatic, product-driven engineer who thrives in high-velocity startup environments

 - Bonus: Experience building applications with AI/LLM integrations, or exposure to ITSM/enterprise workflows. Taking products from 0 to 1

What We Offer

 - Build foundational product features for an AI-first enterprise platform

 - The opportunity to take ownership of critical systems that scale to millions of users

 - A culture that values craftsmanship, autonomy, and technical excellence

 - Competitive compensation, equity, and benefits package

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office, where you’ll be side-by-side with the founding team in a supportive, collaborative setting. Our team works on-site five days a week, growing and building together, and the location is easy to reach with plenty of public transportation options.



As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at is: $160,000—$230,000 USD

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



About the Role:
We’re looking for a Staff Software Engineer who thrives at the intersection of AI systems design, large-scale distributed infrastructure, and product innovation. You’ll lead technical initiatives that bring AI-native capabilities to life—building resilient, scalable systems that integrate LLMs, data-driven automation, and graph intelligence into enterprise workflows.

This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who codes deeply, mentors effectively, and loves shaping technical strategy in an AI-first environment.



Responsibilities:

 - AI-Driven Systems Engineering
   
   - Design and develop AI-native architectures that embed LLMs and intelligent automation into ITSM and CMDB workflows.
   
   - Leverage LLMs, embeddings, and retrieval pipelines to power natural language interfaces, knowledge synthesis, and reasoning within our platform.
   
   - Collaborate with product, design, and data teams to infuse AI into every layer of the product experience—from backend systems to user-facing features.
   
   Technical Ownership
   
   - Own complex systems end-to-end: design, architecture, deployment, and production reliability.
   
   - Drive architectural decisions balancing near-term speed with long-term scalability and maintainability.
   
   - Help define our AI-first engineering stack and patterns for prompt orchestration, model integration, and continuous learning.
   
   Hands-On Engineering
   
   - Write clean, scalable, and testable code that pushes the boundaries of AI-enabled systems.
   
   - Design and optimize distributed, event-driven systems and APIs that handle data and inference at scale.
   
   - Conduct deep-dive code reviews and performance tuning across high-traffic, production environments.
   
   Leadership and Mentorship
   
   - Lead by example—uplifting code quality, fostering a culture of experimentation, and mentoring engineers in AI-augmented workflows.
   
   - Champion best practices in AI tooling adoption (e.g., Cursor, Windsurf, Claude) to enhance team productivity and creativity.

Qualifications:

 - 7+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a record of delivering large-scale, high-impact systems.

 - Deep expertise in distributed systems, cloud-native architecture, and API design.

 - Hands-on experience with or strong interest in LLMs, vector databases, embeddings, or AI agent frameworks.

 - Familiarity with prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and AI-powered developer tools.

 - Experience mentoring engineers and guiding cross-functional teams toward technical excellence.

 - Strong proficiency in one or more modern programming languages (e.g., Go, Python, Rust, or Java).

 - Knowledge of Kubernetes, event-driven systems, and CI/CD best practices.

 - A builder’s mindset: eager to explore, prototype, and ship fast.

What We Offer:

 - Build foundational product features for an AI-first enterprise platform

 - The opportunity to take ownership of critical systems that scale to millions of users

 - A culture that values craftsmanship, autonomy, and technical excellence

 - Competitive compensation, equity, and benefits package

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office, where you’ll be side-by-side with the founding team in a supportive, collaborative setting. Our team works on-site five days a week, growing and building together, and the location is easy to reach with plenty of public transportation options.



As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran sta

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.

About the Role

We’re looking for a Principal Software Engineer, Full-Stack AI to own the end-to-end technical vision for how intelligence is designed, built, and experienced across our platform - from data ingestion and model reasoning to APIs, user interfaces, and real-world operational impact.

This is a deeply hands-on role for a senior technical leader who thrives at the intersection of AI systems, distributed infrastructure, and product-grade software engineering. You will architect and ship production AI systems, build scalable backend and data platforms, and work across the stack to ensure AI capabilities are observable, trustworthy, and intuitive for enterprise users.

You’ll help us define what “applied, full-stack AI” means at Standard Template Labs: designing reasoning pipelines, operationalizing LLMs and agents, shaping human-in-the-loop experiences, and building the platform primitives that allow intelligence to be embedded - not bolted on - across every workflow. You’ll mentor senior engineers, influence product direction, and help establish an engineering culture where AI, systems design, and user experience are tightly integrated.




RESPONSIBILITIES


AI-NATIVE ARCHITECTURE & TECHNICAL STRATEGY

 - Architect the core intelligence layer of the platform, spanning data ingestion, embeddings, retrieval, graph reasoning, agents, and real-time inference.

 - Define how LLMs and predictive models integrate across backend services, APIs, and user-facing experiences.

 - Identify high-impact opportunities where generative, predictive, or autonomous AI can eliminate operational toil, improve system understanding, or enhance decision-making.

 - Lead architectural decisions around model selection, evaluation, fine-tuning, and inference infrastructure (custom vs OSS vs managed APIs).

 - Establish best practices for AI-first engineering, including prompt and schema design, context assembly, evaluators, guardrails, observability, and continuous model monitoring.

 - Partner with product and leadership to align AI capabilities with customer outcomes, trust requirements, and long-term platform strategy.


FULL-STACK APPLIED AI DEVELOPMENT

 - Build end-to-end AI-powered features - from backend reasoning services to APIs and user-facing workflows.

 - Design and implement production-grade LLM and agent workflows, including automated enrichment, anomaly explanation, topology discovery, change impact analysis, and natural language querying.

 - Develop scalable backend systems for high-throughput inference, embedding generation, vector search, and graph traversal.

 - Collaborate on or directly contribute to frontend experiences that make AI outputs understandable, actionable, and debuggable for users (e.g., explanations, confidence signals, provenance, and feedback loops).

 - Implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines and hybrid search systems that combine structured data, graphs, and unstructured context.

 - Write clean, well-structured, production-quality code—and champion AI-assisted development tools (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) to improve velocity and correctness.

 - Continuously evaluate emerging AI frameworks, agent runtimes, orchestration tools, and model APIs, integrating them where they drive real user value.


DATA, INFRASTRUCTURE & PLATFORM FOUNDATIONS

 - Design data models and pipelines that support learning, reasoning, and traceability across the platform.

 - Build and evolve distributed systems that are observable, fault-tolerant, and cost-efficient under AI workloads.

 - Partner with infrastructure and DevOps teams to shape deployment, scaling, monitoring, and rollback strategies for

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



About the Role

Our team is currently seeking AI Engineers that will design, build, and deploy machine learning systems that form the intelligence layer of our ITSM platform. You will work on problems such as natural language understanding of IT tickets, dynamic knowledge extraction, intelligent workflow automation, and predictive insights from complex IT infrastructure data. This is a hands-on engineering role with the opportunity to define the AI foundation of a company from day one.



Responsibilities

 - Research, prototype, and productionize machine learning models for ITSM use cases (ticket triage, incident prediction, workflow recommendations, CMDB inference).

 - Build scalable training and inference pipelines that integrate seamlessly into the core platform.

 - Experiment with LLMs, embeddings, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for IT knowledge and workflow automation.

 - Partner with engineers and product managers to define AI-first features that differentiate our platform.

 - Evaluate performance, optimize models, and ensure reliability, scalability, and cost-efficiency of AI systems.

 - Stay current with advances in machine learning, LLMs, and AI infrastructure, and apply them pragmatically.

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, AI/ML, or related field; PhD is a plus.

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals (Python preferred) and experience with ML frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.

 - Experience with one or more of: natural language processing (NLP), large language models (LLMs), information retrieval, graph ML, or time-series forecasting.

 - Understanding of modern MLOps practices: model versioning, deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management.

 - Comfort working in a fast-paced startup environment with end-to-end ownership.

 - Bonus: experience applying AI in enterprise software or ITSM contexts.

What We Offer

 - Build foundational product features for an AI-first enterprise platform

 - The opportunity to take ownership of critical systems that scale to millions of users

 - A culture that values craftsmanship, autonomy, and technical excellence

 - Competitive compensation, equity, and benefits package

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office, where you’ll be side-by-side with the founding team in a supportive, collaborative setting. Our team works on-site five days a week, growing and building together, and the location is easy to reach with plenty of public transportation options.



As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at is: $160,000—$230,000 USD.

source_jobs.title: IT Operations & Trust Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Standard Template Labs is a stealth-mode, AI-native startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



Role Overview

We’re looking for a hands-on, security-minded IT Operations Engineer who can own the reliability, compliance, and trust posture of our internal systems. You’ll manage everything from user provisioning and device security to compliance automation and continuous monitoring through platforms like Vanta.

This role blends traditional IT operations with modern trust and compliance automation: you’ll keep systems secure and compliant, maintain integrations across HR, Finance, and Engineering, and ensure the company is always audit-ready for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and AI-trust frameworks.



Key Responsibilities

Systems & Identity Management

 - Administer and support core SaaS applications: Google Workspace, Notion, GitHub, Linear, Apple ABM, Okta/SSO, OnePassword, OpenAI/GPT, Claude, DocuSign, Ashby, Gusto, Ramp, LinkedIn Recruiter, BuiltIn.

 - Manage user onboarding/offboarding, MFA, and access control through centralized identity management and SSO.

 - Maintain device management (MDM) policies for encryption, patching, and asset tracking.

Security & Trust Automation

 - Operate and maintain the Vanta (or equivalent) compliance automation platform to monitor controls and evidence freshness.

 - Ensure continuous compliance across SOC 2, ISO 27001, and security frameworks — own control remediation and auditor readiness.

 - Oversee security controls such as MFA enforcement, privileged access audits, and device encryption coverage.

 - Partner with leadership to maintain the company’s Trust Report/Trust Center, including up-to-date documentation, certificates, and vendor reviews.

Automation & Workflow Engineering

 - Build and maintain automations for onboarding/offboarding, data syncs, and system integrations using low-code tools or APIs.

 - Document recurring processes and technical playbooks in Notion for scalability and audit traceability.

 - Identify and automate manual compliance evidence collection tasks to improve efficiency.

Compliance & Vendor Management

 - Support third-party risk management by reviewing vendor SOC 2 reports, tracking renewal dates, and maintaining risk records.

 - Conduct quarterly access reviews and coordinate with HR and Finance to ensure alignment with security policies.

 - Generate monthly dashboards on system uptime, compliance status, and control health for executive visibility.

End-User Support & Enablement

 - Provide Tier 2/3 support for hardware, network, and application issues.

 - Manage Wi-Fi and local network configurations at the NYC office.

 - Lead security awareness training and coach employees on best practices.

Qualifications

 - 5 + years in IT Operations, Systems Administration, or Security Engineering.

 - Experience with Google Workspace and modern SaaS management across departments.

 - Strong understanding of SSO, MFA, identity and access management (IAM).

 - Working knowledge of compliance automation tools (Vanta, Drata, Tugboat, Secureframe or equivalent).

 - Hands-on experience with MDM solutions (Jamf, Intune, or Kandji).

 - Familiarity with SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / NIST principles and trust reporting.

 - Scripting or automation skills (Python, Zapier, Google Apps Script or similar) a plus.

 - Excellent communication and documentation skills; proactive mindset for security and compliance.
   
   

As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated y

source_jobs.title: Sr. Software Engineer – Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As we scale our SaaS platform to support increasingly complex enterprise environments, our graph-based data architecture is becoming a critical strategic pillar. We’re looking for a Senior Engineer with deep experience building and scaling graph-centric, multi-tenant data systems for large-scale SaaS products.

In this role, you’ll own the evolution of our graph data tier—designing performant, secure, and scalable graph models and storage strategies that enable rich relationships, deep querying, and intelligent insights across tenants.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role where you’ll shape the technical foundation of how we represent, store, traverse, and analyze interconnected enterprise data at scale.




RESPONSIBILITIES


GRAPH DATA ARCHITECTURE

 - Architect, evolve, and optimize our multi-tenant graph data model, including node/edge schema design, relationship modeling, partitioning, and traversal strategies.

 - Define and implement tenancy models for graph storage—balancing shared-graph vs. tenant-isolated subgraphs, hybrid partitioning, or database-per-tenant graph deployments.

 - Develop data architecture for graph sharding, indexing, and high-throughput traversal, ensuring low-latency queries even as topology size and complexity scale.


BACKEND & DATA LAYER ENGINEERING

 - Build backend services that interact heavily with datastores, including APIs, data-access layers, ingestion workflows, and graph mutation/query pipelines.

 - Optimize query performance through indexing improvements, caching of subgraphs, tuned traversal patterns, and distributed graph execution.


MULTI-TENANCY & PLATFORM SCALABILITY

 - Own decision-making around multi-tenant graph architecture: tenant boundaries, access control models, resource isolation, and performance fairness.

 - Implement tooling for onboarding, migrations, tenant provisioning, graph expansion, and lifecycle management.

 - Collaborate across product, infrastructure, and security teams to ensure the graph layer meets enterprise SLAs, security, and compliance requirements.


TECHNICAL LEADERSHIP

 - Establish standards around modeling, relationship semantics, data governance, and observability for graph workloads.

 - Build visibility into performance and health—metrics, dashboards, anomaly detection, traversal cost profiling, etc.

 - Mentor engineers in database fundamentals, scalable SaaS data patterns, and distributed systems thinking.


QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience in production SaaS environments.

 - Hands-on experience designing, scaling, or operating multi-tenancy databases in production.

 - Strong understanding of graph modeling, schema design for nodes/edges, indexing, and query/traversal optimization.

 - Proficiency in a modern backend language (Go, Python, Java, Node.js, etc.).

 - Solid grounding in distributed systems concepts, partitioning, caching, and performance optimization.

 - Experience making architectural decisions in fast-changing, high-scale environments.


PREFERRED

 - Experience scaling graph-backed SaaS products for enterprise customers with large, complex topologies.

 - Familiarity with distributed graph engines, distributed SQL, NoSQL, or horizontally scalable datastores.

 - Experience building observability for graph workloads: query heatmaps, traversal metrics, cardinality analysis.

 - Prior experience with major re-architecture efforts—e.g., moving from relational to graph, multi-tenant transitions, or large-scale migrations.


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

 - You will define and deliver enterprise ready, graph-first architecture that powers the next generatio

source_jobs.title: Front-End Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



The Role

We are seeking a talented Front-End Engineer - AI-First to join our small but growing engineering team. In this role, you’ll own the user-facing product: deliver polished, high-performance interfaces, leverage AI-driven tools to accelerate development, and partner with product and design to turn complex enterprise workflows into intuitive front-end experiences.

What You’ll Do

 - Build responsive, accessible, and scalable UIs using modern front-end technologies (e.g. React, Next.js, TypeScript).

 - Develop and maintain a shared component library or design system, supporting consistency across the product.

 - Integrate with backend APIs / services (REST / SSE / GraphQL) to fetch and render data; ensure smooth communication between frontend and backend

 - Use AI-first tooling (e.g. AI-assisted code generation, automated refactoring, AI-assisted testing / linting / code review) to boost developer productivity and maintain high code quality. (Given our AI-savvy culture — this is core to the role.)

 - Collaborate closely with Product and Design teams to translate UX/wireframes into production-ready, user-friendly features.

 - Optimize performance, cross-browser compatibility, and accessibility (responsive layouts, fast load times, smooth interactions).

 - Write clean, maintainable, testable code; participate in code reviews; help define front-end architecture and best practices.

What We’re Looking For

Required / Core Skills

 - Several years of professional front-end engineering experience (or equivalent), with hands-on experience in modern JavaScript/TypeScript frameworks (e.g. React, Next.js)

 - Strong proficiency in HTML, CSS (or CSS-in-JS / styling solutions), responsive design, and front-end fundamentals (DOM, browser behavior, layout, performance).

 - Experience integrating with APIs (REST / GraphQL) and working alongside backend teams.

 - Experience with realtime, streaming AI-first components.

 - Good understanding of UI/UX principles, and comfort collaborating with designers to implement wireframes/mockups.

 - High code quality standards — clean, maintainable, testable code; familiarity with version control (e.g. Git), testing, CI/CD workflows.

 - Desire to embrace AI-augmented engineering workflows — comfortable exploring / adopting AI tools to accelerate UI development, refactoring, testing, or component generation.

Nice-to-Have / Preferred

 - Experience building conversational or chat-based applications, including managing complex multi-turn conversation state and dynamic message flows, similar to ChatGPT-style interactions.

 - Experience building or contributing to design systems / component libraries

 - Experience with TypeScript (or willingness to adopt it)

 - Familiarity with performance optimization, accessibility (a11y), cross-browser support, and responsive design at scale

 - Experience working in a fast-moving startup / AI-driven product environment

 - Strong communication skills — able to articulate UI constraints, tradeoffs, and collaborate across cross-functional teams


WHY YOU’LL ENJOY THIS ROLE

 - You’ll shape the entire front-end experience of a next-gen SaaS product — from UI design to deployment

 - You’ll be at the forefront of a modern “AI-first” engineering culture: using AI tools to 10× your productivity, ship faster, and craft better user experiences

 - You’ll get to work closely with Product and Design, influencing how complex features are surfaced and used by real customers

 - You’ll help build scalable, maintainable UI architecture that will grow with our product



As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind.

source_jobs.title: Senior UX Designer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is a stealth-mode, AI-native startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.

About the Role

You will lead the design of an entirely new category of enterprise software—one where workflows are co-created with AI, interfaces adapt intelligently, and complex IT operations become intuitive, explorable, and automated.

We’re looking for someone who has mastered the fundamentals of UX and is excited to define what “good” looks like for AI-native enterprise UX in a world where traditional patterns are being rewritten.

Responsibilities

 - Lead UX strategy and vision for an AI-first enterprise product

 - Design intuitive, intelligent workflows that seamlessly blend human decision-making with AI assistance.

 - Translate complex technical concepts, data models, and IT processes into clear, elegant UX patterns.

 - Create wireframes, prototypes, and end-to-end design flows for enterprise-grade software with consumer-grade simplicity.

 - Define interaction patterns for AI-driven experiences, including prompts, conversational flows, recommendations, and adaptive UI states.

 - Partner closely with founders, engineering, and product to shape product direction from 0→1.

 - Conduct user research and usability testing, synthesizing insights into actionable design decisions.

 - Advocate for user needs while balancing technical constraints and business priorities.

 - Build and maintain a scalable design system for an AI-native, multi-surface product environment.

 - Iterate rapidly in a high-velocity startup environment, embracing ambiguity and continuous learning.

Qualifications

 - 5-10 years of UX/Product Design experience, at a top enterprise SaaS company. Portfolio demonstrating exceptional interaction design, systems thinking, workflow design, and problem-solving for complex products

 - Mastery of Figma and modern prototyping tools (Framer, ProtoPie, Principle, etc.)

 - Deep familiarity with AI-powered products and emerging interaction patterns for LLM-driven interfaces.

 - Strong communication skills with the ability to clearly articulate design rationale across disciplines.

 - Comfort operating in fast-paced environments and driving high-quality work under rapid iteration cycles.

 - Comfortable with rapid iteration in a fast-paced, high-velocity startup environment

 - Bonus: experience in service design, design research, information architecture, or systems modeling.

 - Background in IT operations, ITSM, DevOps, or complex enterprise domains is a plus.

What We Offer

 - Define the UX patterns of AI-first enterprise software

 - Work directly with founders in a high-growth startup

 - Competitive salary + equity

 - Full health, dental, and vision benefits

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office alongside the founding team in a focused, collaborative environment. We're in-office five days a week, moving quickly and building together—with easy access to public transportation.

As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.

We’re looking for a hands-on Product Manager who excels at working directly with enterprise customers and turning messy, real-world problems into shipped product. You’ll spend time in the field with customers, deeply understand how IT, support, and operations teams actually work, and transform ambiguous needs into clear product direction. Partnering closely with engineering and design, you’ll lead the delivery of AI-powered features at scale, across large, enterprise organizations.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Work directly with enterprise customers to observe real workflows, uncover friction and failure points, and identify opportunities where AI can meaningfully improve outcomes.

 - Take ambiguous, imperfect inputs - customer conversations, operational data, edge cases - and synthesize them into clear product requirements, PRDs, and prioritized roadmaps.

 - Own features end-to-end, from problem definition and solution design through build, launch, and iteration.

 - Collaborate tightly with engineering and design to ensure solutions are practical, scalable, and grounded in how customers actually operate.

 - Use customer feedback, product usage, and operational metrics to continuously refine and improve shipped features, balancing speed with quality and reliability.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR


CORE REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven ability to work directly with customers and turn messy, real-world problems into shipped product.

 - 4+ years of product management experience, ideally in enterprise SaaS.

 - Strong instincts for breaking down complex workflows into solvable, well-scoped product opportunities.

 - Comfort working closely with engineers, with a solid understanding of software development concepts and tradeoffs.

 - Excitement about building in an AI-first environment where experimentation, iteration, and learning are core to how products are built.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building AI-powered, automation-heavy, or data-driven products.

 - Familiarity with APIs, distributed systems, and enterprise integrations.

 - Exposure to enterprise deployment, security, compliance, or onboarding challenges.

 - Startup or high-growth experience where shipping matters more than perfection.


WHY THIS ROLE

 - Direct, ongoing access to enterprise customers and ownership over meaningful, high-impact problems.

 - A fast-moving, AI-native environment where you can go from idea to production quickly.

 - Close collaboration with experienced engineering and design partners who value strong product judgment.

 - A role centered on real execution—shipping product, learning from customers, and making tangible improvements every week.



As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at is: $175,000—$250,000 USD

source_jobs.title: Sr. Front-End Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.



The Role

We are seeking a Senior Front-End Engineer (AI-First) to join our small but growing engineering team. In this role, you’ll own and shape the user-facing product end to end: designing and building polished, high-performance interfaces, setting front-end technical direction, and leveraging AI-driven tools to accelerate development. You’ll partner closely with Product, Design, and Backend Engineering to turn complex enterprise workflows into intuitive, scalable front-end experiences.

This is a hands-on senior role with real ownership - ideal for someone who enjoys building, mentoring through example, and raising the bar for front-end quality across the product.

What You’ll Do

 - Own the front-end architecture for core product surfaces, making thoughtful decisions around state management, data fetching, performance, and scalability.

 - Build responsive, accessible, and high-performance UIs using modern front-end technologies (e.g. React, Next.js, TypeScript).

 - Design, develop, and evolve a shared component library / design system, ensuring consistency, usability, and maintainability as the product grows.

 - Collaborate closely with backend engineers to define API contracts (REST / SSE / GraphQL) and ensure smooth, efficient data flow between frontend and backend.

 - Build AI-first, realtime user experiences, including streaming and interactive components that respond dynamically to backend and model outputs.

 - Leverage AI-assisted development tools (code generation, refactoring, testing, linting, review) as a core part of your workflow to increase velocity while maintaining high code quality.

 - Partner deeply with Product and Design to translate UX concepts and wireframes into production-ready features, proactively identifying edge cases, trade-offs, and implementation risks.

 - Drive performance, accessibility, and quality across the front-end stack (load times, responsiveness, cross-browser support, a11y).

 - Set and uphold high engineering standards: write clean, testable code; lead by example in code reviews; help define front-end best practices and patterns.

What We’re Looking For

Required / Core Skills

 - Several years of professional front-end engineering experience (or equivalent), with deep hands-on experience building production applications using modern JavaScript / TypeScript frameworks (e.g. React, Next.js).

 - Strong mastery of HTML, CSS, and modern styling approaches (CSS-in-JS, design tokens, component-based styling), with a deep understanding of browser behavior, layout, and performance.

 - Proven experience owning and evolving complex front-end systems, not just implementing features.

 - Experience integrating with backend APIs (REST / GraphQL / streaming APIs) and collaborating closely with backend teams on system design.

 - Experience building or supporting realtime, streaming, or AI-driven UI components.

 - Strong product and UX instincts - comfortable working with designers and product managers to refine flows, interactions, and edge cases.

 - High standards for code quality and maintainability; experience with testing strategies, CI/CD workflows, and version control (Git).

 - Strong interest in - and comfort with - AI-augmented engineering workflows, using AI tools to accelerate development without sacrificing correctness or quality.

Nice-to-Have / Preferred

 - Experience building conversational or chat-based interfaces, including managing complex client-side state, streaming responses, and dynamic message flows (e.g. ChatGPT-style UIs).

 - Prior ownership of a design system or shared component library used by multiple engineers or teams.

 - Deep experience with Type

source_jobs.title: Sr. Platform Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is an AI-native, seed stage startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.





THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Platform Software Engineer to build the systems, tooling, and internal platform that make engineers dramatically more productive.

This role sits at the intersection of:

 - Platform engineering

 - Developer experience (DevEx)

 - Infrastructure automation

 - Distributed systems

You’ll build the foundation that enables engineers to Ship faster, Deploy safely, Debug quickly & Operate confidently in production

This is a highly hands-on engineering role. You’ll write code, design systems, and shape how engineering works across the company.





WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON





DEVELOPER PRODUCTIVITY & INTERNAL PLATFORM

 - Build and evolve our internal developer platform (IDP)

 - Create self-service tooling that makes spinning up services, environments, and infrastructure effortless

 - Improve local development workflows, testing, deployment ergonomics, and developer feedback loops

 - Reduce operational friction through automation and platform abstractions


CI/CD & RELEASE ENGINEERING

 - Design and maintain scalable CI/CD systems using GitHub Actions &Infrastructure-as-code workflows

 - Improve: Build times, Deployment reliability & Rollback and recovery systems

 - Create deployment workflows that are fast, observable, and boring in production


CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE & KUBERNETES

 - Design and operate cloud-native systems on AWS, EKS, Docker & Kubernetes

 - Improve platform scalability, resilience, and developer onboarding

 - Define service architecture and deployment standards across the engineering organization


OBSERVABILITY & RELIABILITY

 - Own observability across the platform using Datadog, Metrics, Logging and Distributed tracing

 - Build dashboards, alerts, and operational tooling engineers actually use

 - Improve reliability, performance, and operational visibility across services


DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS & PLATFORM SERVICES

 - Work across core infrastructure systems including: PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis & Temporal


ENGINEERING ENABLEMENT

 - Partner closely with backend and AI engineers to improve platform usability

 - Establish engineering standards and platform best practices

 - Make infrastructure feel like a product, not a support function




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience building Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs)

 - Experience in high-growth or early-stage startups

 - Experience with AI/ML infrastructure

 - Experience designing multi-service distributed systems




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Most companies treat platform engineering as support.

We don’t. This team will define how every system is built, every engineer work and how fast we can ship. If we get this right, everything else gets easier.


As an equal opportunity employer, we don’t tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that’s based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at is: $160,000—$250,000 USD.

source_jobs.title: Growth / Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Standard Template Labs is a stealth-mode, AI-native startup reimagining the future of IT Service and Configuration Management. Backed by leading investors, we're leveraging AI, graph-based architecture, and exceptional design to transform how enterprises manage and engage with their technology ecosystems.

About the Role

You will be the first Growth and Marketing hire, defining our voice, shaping our brand, and building the engine that turns a technical product into a story that resonates with enterprise buyers. This is a 0→1 role for someone who can think strategically and execute relentlessly with minimal guidance.

Responsibilities

 - Own and articulate the STLabs brand - our voice, narrative, and positioning.

 - Translate a technically sophisticated, AI-native product into clear, persuasive messaging for technical and executive enterprise audiences.

 - Write and ship high-quality external content: website copy, launch materials, thought leadership, product narratives, and customer-facing collateral.

 - Partner closely with founders, product, and design to ensure our market story stays tightly coupled to what we're actually building.

 - Develop deep fluency in our product, our category, and our customers' problems, and use it to sharpen everything we say.

 - Identify and pursue growth opportunities proactively, testing and iterating quickly to find what works.

Qualifications

 - 2-5 years of experience understanding customers, crafting compelling narratives, and cultivating a strong brand. (Growth, Product Marketing, Consulting etc.)

 - Exceptional writing and communication skills - you can make complex, technical ideas clear, credible, and compelling.

 - A strong instinct for brand and narrative that clearly communicates our differentiation and resonates with customer pain points.

 - Demonstrated ability to learn technical products deeply and translate them for different audiences.

 - A proactive, self-directed operator who thrives without an established playbook and drives outcomes independently.

 - Unmatched attention to detail without sacrificing the velocity of an early-stage startup.

What We Offer

 - Define the brand and growth motion of a new category of AI-first enterprise software from the ground up.

 - Competitive salary + equity.

 - Full health, dental, and vision benefits.

 - Work from our Flatiron District, Manhattan office alongside the founding team in a focused, collaborative environment. We're in-office five days a week, moving quickly and building together—with easy access to public transportation.

As an equal opportunity employer, we don't tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind. Whether that's based on race, ethnicity, age, gender identity, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, veteran status or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state or local laws. The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role is: $130,000 - $180,000 USD.

notes: AI-native startup with strong focus on engineering and product development
